title: lichen_l2_input holds rx_mutex across net_pkt_alloc which may block
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 445-538: rx_mutex is held from line 445 until line 538, spanning net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer() (line 520) and net_pkt_write() (line 530). Both may block waiting for memory. This could delay other RX processing if multiple packets arrive rapidly.

However: LoRa at SF10 has ~100ms minimum packet time, so packet bursts are unlikely. The mutex protects rx_ipv6_buf which is shared. Would need double-buffering to improve, which adds complexity.

Verdict: Acceptable for current LoRa data rates. Flag for reconsideration if moving to faster radios.
